\noindent We use Equations 5.29 and 5.30 to construct a prediction interval for total revenue for the year under audit. The annual expectation is the sum of the 12 monthly expectations.

\noindent The prediction standard error of the annual total is calculated directly from the design matrix for the hold-out observations. This accounts for the covariance between the monthly expectations caused by using the same estimated regression coefficients.

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\partopsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}%
}}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
# Residual degrees of freedom
df_res <- ussteamco.mod.5$df.residual

# Monthly predictions
monthly_predictions <- pred_df$fit

# Annual expectation
annual_prediction <- sum(monthly_predictions)

# Construct the design matrix for the hold-out observations
X_hold <- model.matrix(
  formula(ussteamco.mod.5),
  data = USSteamCoHold
)

# Vector of ones for summing the 12 monthly predictions
one <- rep(1, nrow(X_hold))

# Variance component from uncertainty in the estimated coefficients
var_mean_annual <- as.numeric(
  t(one) %*% X_hold %*% vcov(ussteamco.mod.5) %*% t(X_hold) %*% one
)

# Variance component from future residual variation
sigma2 <- summary(ussteamco.mod.5)$sigma^2
var_future_annual <- nrow(X_hold) * sigma2

# Prediction variance and prediction standard error of the annual total
annual_var <- var_mean_annual + var_future_annual
annual_se <- sqrt(annual_var)

# t-score for a 99% prediction interval
t_score <- qt(0.995, df = df_res)

# 99% prediction interval for the annual total
annual_lower <- annual_prediction - t_score * annual_se
annual_upper <- annual_prediction + t_score * annual_se

# Print results
cat("Variance from coefficient uncertainty:", var_mean_annual, "\n")
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
Variance from coefficient uncertainty: 14886605275947
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
cat("Variance from future residual variation:", var_future_annual, "\n")
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
Variance from future residual variation: 25097531113309
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
cat("99% Prediction Interval for Annual Revenue:\n")
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
99% Prediction Interval for Annual Revenue:
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
cat("Lower Bound:", round(annual_lower, 0), "\n")
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
Lower Bound: 181291634
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
cat("Annual Prediction:", round(annual_prediction, 0), "\n")
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
Annual Prediction: 198680684
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
cat("Upper Bound:", round(annual_upper, 0), "\n")
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
Upper Bound: 216069735
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
cat("Annual prediction standard error:", round(annual_se, 0), "\n")
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
Annual prediction standard error: 6323301
\end{Verbatim}
\endgroup
\par\addvspace{\topsep}

\noindent The first variance component, \ttblue{var\_mean\_annual}, captures the uncertainty in the estimated regression coefficients. The second component, \ttblue{var\_future\_annual}, captures the future residual variation of the 12 monthly observations. The latter is calculated under the assumption that the future residual errors are uncorrelated.

\noindent Adjust the March prediction based on the corroborated evidence for the winter conditions and disruptions.
